title: How could you not like EVA
display_title: How could you not like EVA
wikitext:
[]: How could you not like EVA             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Sat Jul 15, 2006 6:47 pm Of all of my friends only 2 of them like evangelion. So I had like 15 people over my house and so we watched all of evangelion. None of them liked it I got comments like "Hellsing is better" "that was the worst anime ever. genma wars was better!" "That was a waste of paper" "why do people like that show" and my fav " that was so horible it wasn anime. it was an american creation. It has to be!"

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 15, 2006 6:58 pm My friend EVA is full of agnst, pain, darkly human weakness, and very very little hope and comady. If you don't like the dark, if you don't feel a lot of dipresion and agnst that EVA is literly built from then its not for you. Nothing wrong witht that, after all it is a question of personal taste.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Sat Jul 15, 2006 7:05 pm I know but they insulted me and then they didnt beleive that this forum existed. When I showed them the forum they said we were all losers.

[]:             Quote Post by System Shocker » Sat Jul 15, 2006 7:42 pm a lot of the futuristic technologies in the show don't really have that much to them. There are some good designs but a lot of the stuff in the show is 3rd grade science and sort of dumb. It's a little weird hearing them talk about it in the Japanese dub, but the English voice actors were pretty good with it although they still had no idea what they were talking about. Sometimes it's a little awkward not understanding how things work.

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:25 pm Yea sur hellsing iz betta cuz dey hav guns. lol

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:52 pm Your friends don't like Eva. So what? You can't twist someone's arm and make them watch a show and like it. People have to find Eva themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Jul 15, 2006 11:29 pm If you see Eva as just a mecha show, you probably won't like it. From that perspective, it really isn't very good. The brilliance of the series comes (I think) from the strength of the other stories it tells, above and beyond the literal one of the world's destruction. Most of the series is about Anno's depression, and EoE is a direct and harsh criticism of otaku culture, in many ways. In addition, the characters, not the action, really drive the plot. That combination of depth and action is pretty rare -- Blade Runner comes to mind, and divides people in much the same way. Not being prepared to look beyond the surface, or invest emotionally in the characters, will almost inevitably lead to disappointment in the series. The creators, after all, did name it "Gospel for a New Century."

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Jul 16, 2006 12:47 am What you have to remember is that no matter how good something is, there will always be someone who hates it just as no matter how bad something is there will always be someone who loves it. Also my freind who hates anime likes eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 16, 2006 2:01 am drinian wrote: If you see Eva as just a mecha show, you probably won't like it. From that perspective, it really isn't very good.

[]:             Quote Post by Light of the Dawn » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:01 am WTF? Weird comments. Here in Israel every anime-lover has watched NGE and most of them liked it very much... How could someone compate between NGE and Hellsing? That's just stupid. Hellsing has it's own story and NGE has it's own story. (BTW, I think NGE is MUCH better)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:17 am Different strokes for different folks - visit 4chan and you'll see a lot of the "It's cool not to like something with a big reputation". My wife watched ep#4 over my shoulder, and was interested enough to watch the whole thing (though doesn't understand the obsession). I loaned the Collection 0:1 DVD to a friend, and he just said "It looks like a lot of excuses for giant robot fights." Never mind episode 4 (let alone the more subtle clues about an Eva's true nature). I think you need a predisposition to like anime, and have the right set of geeky personality flaws.

[]:             Quote Post by badc0de » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:20 am Well... everyone I know who has watched NGE liked or loved it very much, except my brother, after about 15 minutes watching episode 1 he went away saying something like "god damned Japanese baby cartoons!". If he would know, what he is missing Ok, my mistake was probably that I selected the German dub and not original dub with subtitles. :? At least this has something positive: If I want to get rid of him, I only have to grab one of the DVDs and he will screamingly run away! :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:21 am Somehow when you watch Evangelion for the first time you may not like it, it's too hyterical. I think it depends on the kind of stuff you like. If you like Gundam, Eva may not be for you. To discover the real interest of Eva I think it would better to watch it a couple of times, savour it and you'll find it interesting. But at some moment like the two final episodes it's really quite philosophical and can really drive poeple mad. But I really can't understand why your family think Eva is that bad, Evangelionfreak.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:25 am tinmeigut wrote: Somehow when you watch Evangelion for the first time you may not like it, it's too hyterical. I think it depends on the kind of stuff you like. If you like Gundam, Eva may not be for you. To discover the real interest of Eva I think it would better to watch it a couple of times, savour it and you'll find it interesting. But at some moment like the two final episodes it's really quite philosophical and can really drive poeple mad. But I really can't understand why your family think Eva is that bad, Evangelionfreak.

[]:             Quote Post by Light of the Dawn » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:32 am Legend of the Past wrote: tinmeigut wrote: Somehow when you watch Evangelion for the first time you may not like it, it's too hyterical. I think it depends on the kind of stuff you like. If you like Gundam, Eva may not be for you. To discover the real interest of Eva I think it would better to watch it a couple of times, savour it and you'll find it interesting. But at some moment like the two final episodes it's really quite philosophical and can really drive poeple mad. But I really can't understand why your family think Eva is that bad, Evangelionfreak.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:37 am Just because they WEREN'T geeks doesn't mean they CAN'T be geeks. Given enough video games and anime, they can easily become geeks. Geeks are people obsessed with anime\games. I think it's safe to assume everyone here are geeks.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:42 am Legend of the Past wrote: Geeks are people obsessed with anime\games. I think it's safe to assume everyone here are geeks.

[]:             Quote Post by Light of the Dawn » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:48 am Legend of the Past wrote: Just because they WEREN'T geeks doesn't mean they CAN'T be geeks. Given enough video games and anime, they can easily become geeks. Geeks are people obsessed with anime\games. I think it's safe to assume everyone here are geeks.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 16, 2006 4:00 am Mr. Tines wrote: Legend of the Past wrote: Geeks are people obsessed with anime\games. I think it's safe to assume everyone here are geeks.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Jul 16, 2006 4:14 am I guess this has to do with people's tastes! A lot of people like Eva, and a lot of others dislike it! That is natural! What you like, doesn't mean that your friend or something will like it too!
